reasoning_model_1: The model successfully generates the content '哇！我赢了！太棒了！' matching the script perfectly between 00:00 and 00:03. The delivery adopts an energetic, high-pitched tone consistent with the 'screaming manner' (带尖叫的方式) requested in the prompt. The vocalization conveys excitement and high intensity ('Wow! I won! That's great!') without any electronic artifacts or unnatural pauses. The expression of the paralinguistic feature (screaming/shouting for joy) is integrated naturally into the speech as requested.
reasoning_model_2: The model fails significantly in both content accuracy and paralinguistic execution. Firstly, it deviates from the script by adding '啊啊' (00:01), repeating '太棒了' three times (00:03), and hallucinating entire sentences like '我简直要飞起来了' (00:05) and '这感觉太爽了吧' (00:09). Critically, regarding the 'Non-verbal vocalization' (非语言发声) requirement, instead of performing a scream, the model literally speaks the words 'Scream scream scream' ('尖叫尖叫尖叫') at 00:07. This indicates a complete failure to understand the instruction as a stylistic direction, treating it instead as text to be read.
model_comparison: Model 1 followed the instructions perfectly, delivering the correct script with the requested high-energy 'screaming' manner. Model 2 failed completely; it hallucinated a large amount of text and, most egregiously, read the stage direction 'Scream' out loud as the words '尖叫' instead of modulating its voice to sound like a scream. Model 2 receives a score of 0 due to the severe content accuracy and instruction-following failures outlined in the 0-score criteria (content inconsistent, meaning changed, opposite/wrong characteristic). Model 1 is the clear winner.
